free_text: 96Zr(n,g) measurement at the nTOF facility at CERN
Pole Univ. Leonard de Vinci, Paris La Defense
Giuseppe Taliente, doctoral thesis submitted to
Univ. Gent. Normalization factor of capture yield
applied for SAMMY analysis
CERN nTOF facility.
Protons (7E+12 protons/pulse) were accelerated to
20 GeV/c.
Neutrons produced in a Pb spallation target
were moderated in Pb target in a cooling water
(5.8 cm thick) surrounding the target.
Full flux was evaluated based on combined measurements
from SiMoN [6Li(n,t) reaction] and PTB [235U(n,f)
reaction] detectors.
Cylindrical (22 mm diameter, 1.3 mm thick) ZrO2 powder
(3.398 g, 4.208E-3 Zr atoms/b) enriched to 96Zr
(58.5%) pressed to a pellet and encapsulated in an Al
can (0.1 mm-thick wall and mass of ~300 mg).
Additional Au and Pb samples of the same diameter
for repeated neutron flux measurements and background
runs.
Two C6D6 liquid scintillators placed
perpendicular to the neutron beam at a distance of
~3 cm from the beam axis, and 9.2 cm upstream of the
sample position.
For relative neutron flux measurement (SiMoN)
during irradiation
PTB 235U parallel plate fission chamber for
neutron flux measurement
Flight path length of 185 m.
On+ED
On. Major revisions in 002.
On. Minor revisions in 002.
Unnormalized yield with background
The areal density of Zr+O+Al is 1.523E-2 atoms/b
which was used in the resonance analysis.
Absolute normalization to the saturated 4.9 eV
capture resonance of 197Au
Statistical uncertainty
Energy dependence of neutron flux and fraction
of the neutron beam covered by the sample (2%)
Systematic uncertainty due to PHWT (2%)
By author:
- A normalization factor of 0.660(7) was used in SAMMY
(SAMMY manual: "Normalization is applied first,
followed by background corrections, so that the
backgrounds are not multiplied by the normalization")
- The energy values correspond to the middle of the
bins.
- The various binnings used should be ok for resonance
analysis.
Data received from G.Tagliente via E.Dupont
On. Received from E.Dupont by email
On. THICKNESS updated and SAMPLE added.
On. ENDBIB(N2) updated.
no adjustment for neutron widths
Neutron widths without further adjustment
SAMMY with Reich-Moore approximation with
- 6.8 fm for potential scattering radius.
Corrected for
- capture events in the detectors or in surrounding
materials due to neutrons scattered from the sample
- in-beam gamma-rays produced in the spallation target
- capture events in the Al can of Zr sample
- ambient background in the experimental area
